text: Religion should welcome reason; faith isn't fragile when questions are allowed.
Science gives us tools, but morality guides how we use them; freedom to doubt is essential.
I chose reason over faith because inquiry matters more than certainty.
Morality doesn't require religion; compassion and evidence can walk hand in hand.
Freedom to speak and to question dogma protects believers and nonbelievers alike.
Science and reason help us separate tradition from truth without dismissing anyone's faith.
